ThingDef.label: M1916 37mm gun
ThingDef.description: A light infantry gun firing compact explosive shells in direct fire. It needs a crew and prepared 37mm ammunition.

ThingDef.label: Ironfang M1911
ThingDef.description: Hartmann's masterpiece, a gun handed down from grandfathers, and a gun like the heart of Vessenica. A rugged semi-automatic service pistol with a heavy cartridge and fast follow-up shots.

ThingDef.label: M1A1 Harrington
ThingDef.description: A rugged submachine gun with a heavy bolt and a fast cyclic rate. It fires hard-hitting pistol cartridges in controlled bursts.

ThingDef.label: M3A1 submachine gun
ThingDef.description: A compact stamped-metal submachine gun built for simple production and steady close-range automatic fire.

ThingDef.label: M9A1 recoilless launcher
ThingDef.description: A shoulder-fired recoilless rocket launcher for attacking armor, emplacements, and clustered hard targets. It must be loaded before firing, can be operated alone or by a two-pawn crew, and fires a shaped-charge M6A3 HEAT rocket in a flat, visible line.

ThingDef.label: M1917 heavy machine gun
ThingDef.description: A water-cooled heavy machine gun fed by a long ammunition belt. It is too heavy to use as a carried weapon and is intended for fixed defensive mounts.

ThingDef.label: 3-inch Hawkins mortar
ThingDef.description: A simple infantry mortar firing high-explosive bombs in a high arc. It requires a crew and prepared shells.

ThingDef.label: M1 81mm mortar
ThingDef.description: A battalion infantry mortar firing heavy 81mm bombs in a high arc. It requires a crew and prepared M43 HE shells.

ThingDef.label: Graylock M1892
ThingDef.description: A Graylock Arsenal production of the Berg-Larsen rifle, a bolt-action design with a unique side-loading magazine. Famous for its smooth action.

ThingDef.label: Frontier Arms M1897
ThingDef.description: A rugged pump-action trench shotgun built for close fighting. Its tubular magazine and fast action make it devastating at short range.

ThingDef.label: M1 Hartwell
ThingDef.description: A semi-automatic battle rifle fed by an en-bloc clip. It gives up a little deliberate precision for rapid, powerful follow-up fire. In later stories, the adoption of small-caliber high-velocity rounds was delayed by the Dark Hartwell Order.

ThingDef.label: M1D Hartwell
ThingDef.description: A scoped M1 Hartwell variant built for accurate semi-automatic fire at extended range. It trades some close handling for steadier long-range shooting.

ThingDef.label: M1918A2 HAR
ThingDef.description: A heavy automatic rifle designed to walk with the infantry line. Its slow-fire setting keeps short bursts controllable, while sharpshooters can brace into a faster suppressive mode.

ThingDef.label: M1919A6 light machine gun
ThingDef.description: A portable belt-fed machine gun adapted from the Hartmann M1919. Heavy for an infantry weapon, but able to lay down sustained suppressive fire.

ThingDef.label: Graylock M1903
ThingDef.description: A reliable and accurate bolt-action rifle that served for decades. Highly prized for its precision.

ThingDef.label: Graylock M1903A4
ThingDef.description: A scoped M1903 variant prepared for deliberate long-range fire. It keeps the familiar action of the service rifle while stretching its effective reach.

ThingDef.label: Graylock M1903 Whitmore device
ThingDef.description: An M1903 rifle fitted with a Whitmore conversion device. It uses lighter ammunition instead of full-power rifle cartridges, allowing fast semi-automatic fire.

ThingDef.label: M1902 officer saber
ThingDef.description: A light ceremonial service saber carried by officers. It looks proper on parade, but its narrow blade and light fittings make it a poor battlefield weapon.
